Exploring the impact of elevation on consumers with a PIECEWISE SEM

Author

Carla Behringer

General Preparation pre-analyses

Load required libraries

library(ggplot2)
library(piecewiseSEM)
library(lmerTest)
library(TMB) # required for glmmTMB
library(glmmTMB) # fitting a betaregression model
library(DHARMa) # residual diagnostics for glmm models
library(performance)
library(writexl)

Loading datasets & preparing data

# dataframe with already transformed variables
BugNetsitedataelev_transformed <- read.csv("data/BugNet_sites_elev_transformed.csv")
# dataframe without transformed variables
BugNetsitedataelev_filtered <- read.csv("data/BugNet_sites_elev_selectedVar.csv")

df_elevGradSites <- BugNetsitedataelev_transformed

# add squared elevation term for quadratic relationship of Pathogen damage and elevation after scaling it
df_elevGradSites$elevation_scaled <- scale(df_elevGradSites$elevation_correct)
df_elevGradSites$elevation_scaled <- as.numeric(df_elevGradSites$elevation_scaled)
df_elevGradSites$elevation_c2 <- df_elevGradSites$elevation_scaled^2

z-scale all variables

df_elevGradSites$log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled <- scale(df_elevGradSites$log_Phenylpropanoids)
df_elevGradSites$SLA_scaled <- scale(df_elevGradSites$meanCWM.SLA)
df_elevGradSites$Height_scaled <- scale(df_elevGradSites$meanCWM.Height)

# scale returns a matrix, but piecewiseSEM needs numeric vectors
df_elevGradSites$log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled <- as.numeric(df_elevGradSites$log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled)
df_elevGradSites$SLA_scaled <- as.numeric(df_elevGradSites$SLA_scaled)
df_elevGradSites$Height_scaled <- as.numeric(df_elevGradSites$Height_scaled)

HERBIVORY PIECEWISE SEM

(1) Fitting the individual models

SLA <- lmer(SLA_scaled ~ elevation_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) , data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
Height <- lmer(Height_scaled ~ elevation_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) , data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
Phenolics <- lmer(log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled ~ elevation_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) , data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
herbivory <- lmer(site_logit_herbivory ~ elevation_scaled
              + SLA_scaled + Height_scaled + log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) ,
              data = df_elevGradSites)

(2) Checking model assumptions of the single models

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
# Define a function for residuals vs fitted + QQ plot
check_model <- function(model, model_name = "") {
  par(mfrow = c(1,2))
  
  # Residuals vs Fitted
  plot(fitted(model), resid(model),
       xlab = "Fitted values", ylab = "Residuals",
       main = paste(model_name, "Residuals vs Fitted"))
  lines(lowess(fitted(model), resid(model)), col = "red", lwd = 2)
  
  # QQ plot
  qqnorm(resid(model), main = paste(model_name, "QQ-Plot"))
  qqline(resid(model), col = "red", lwd = 2)
  
  par(mfrow = c(1,1))  # reset layout
}


models <- list(SLA = SLA, Height = Height, Phenolics = Phenolics,
               herbivory = herbivory)

for (nm in names(models)) {
  check_model(models[[nm]], nm)
}
# herbivory model
simres <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = herbivory, n = 1000)
plot(simres) # looks good!
par(mfrow = c(1,1))

(3) Building the piecewise SEM

sem_model <- psem(
  SLA,
  Height,
  Phenolics,
  herbivory
)

(4) Model checks

Checking for missing paths

dSep(sem_model)

  |                                                                            
  |                                                                      |   0%
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

  |                                                                            
  |=======================                                               |  33%
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

  |                                                                            
  |===============================================                       |  67%
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

  |                                                                            
  |======================================================================| 100%
                                     Independ.Claim Test.Type       DF
1                  Height_scaled ~ SLA_scaled + ...      coef 12.97702
2    log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled ~ SLA_scaled + ...      coef 15.20764
3 log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled ~ Height_scaled + ...      coef 16.00000
  Crit.Value   P.Value 
1   1.147248 0.2719912 
2  -1.120281 0.2799749 
3  -1.434499 0.1706868

Checking model fit

fisherC(sem_model)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
  Fisher.C df P.Value
1    8.686  6   0.192

Chi-squared!

Structural Equation Model of sem_model 

Call:
  SLA_scaled ~ elevation_scaled
  Height_scaled ~ elevation_scaled
  log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled ~ elevation_scaled
  site_logit_herbivory ~ elevation_scaled + SLA_scaled + Height_scaled + log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled

    AIC
 218.245

---

     log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled ~ SLA_scaled + ...      coef 15.2076    -1.1203
  log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled ~ Height_scaled + ...      coef 16.0000    -1.4345
  P.Value 
   0.2720 
   0.2800 
   0.1707 

--
Global goodness-of-fit:

Chi-Squared = NA with P-value = NA and on 3 degrees of freedom
Fisher's C = 8.686 with P-value = 0.192 and on 6 degrees of freedom

---
Coefficients:

                     Response                   Predictor Estimate Std.Error
                   SLA_scaled            elevation_scaled  -0.6330    0.1878
                Height_scaled            elevation_scaled  -1.0863    0.1986
  log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled            elevation_scaled   0.7161    0.1808
         site_logit_herbivory            elevation_scaled  -0.0256    0.2794
         site_logit_herbivory                  SLA_scaled   0.2166    0.2130
         site_logit_herbivory               Height_scaled   0.2954    0.2244
         site_logit_herbivory log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled  -0.1732    0.2489
       DF Crit.Value P.Value Std.Estimate    
  17.0000    -3.3713  0.0036      -0.6330  **
  16.2408    -5.4691  0.0000      -1.0863 ***
  10.2502     3.9594  0.0026       0.7161  **
   9.4928    -0.0917  0.9288      -0.0297    
  11.4232     1.0171  0.3302       0.2513    
  10.4326     1.3165  0.2162       0.3427    
  10.3818    -0.6959  0.5018      -0.2010    

  Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05

---
Individual R-squared:

                     Response method Marginal Conditional
                   SLA_scaled   none     0.39        0.39
                Height_scaled   none     0.36        0.91
  log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled   none     0.48        0.56
         site_logit_herbivory   none     0.38        0.54

Interpretation of model summary:

  • Chi2 and Fishers C are non significant

  • no missing paths detected

    • Conclusion: Model is statistically plausible

  • Elevation has strong effects on traits

  • No effects of traits and elevation on herbivory

  • traits and elevation explain 37 % of herbivory which is substantial

    • meaningful variance explained but no single path significant = shared explanatory power

      • Herbivory is influenced by overall plant strategy, not one specific trait???

PATHOGEN PIECEWISE SEM

(1) Fitting the individual models

SLA <- lmer(SLA_scaled ~ elevation_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) , data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
Height <- lmer(Height_scaled ~ elevation_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) , data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
Phenolics <- lmer(log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled ~ elevation_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) , data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
# composite variable for elevation (linear & quadratic term) 
# only for pathogen damage because of the non-linear relationship
elevation_model <- lm(site_logit_pathogen ~ elevation_scaled + elevation_c2, data = df_elevGradSites)
summary(elevation_model)

Call:
lm(formula = site_logit_pathogen ~ elevation_scaled + elevation_c2, 
    data = df_elevGradSites)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-1.29763 -0.97065  0.08535  0.87313  1.93265 

Coefficients:
                 Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)       -3.6307     0.3476 -10.445 1.49e-08 ***
elevation_scaled  -0.8491     0.3035  -2.798  0.01289 *  
elevation_c2      -0.7609     0.2460  -3.093  0.00698 ** 
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 1.124 on 16 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.6793,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.6392 
F-statistic: 16.95 on 2 and 16 DF,  p-value: 0.0001119
elev_linear <- coef(elevation_model)[2] * df_elevGradSites$elevation_scaled
elev_quadratic <- coef(elevation_model)[3] * df_elevGradSites$elevation_c2
elev_effect <- elev_linear + elev_quadratic
df_elevGradSites$elev_effect <- elev_effect
#standardize elev effect
df_elevGradSites$elev_effect_s <- scale(df_elevGradSites$elev_effect)
df_elevGradSites$elev_effect_s <- as.numeric(df_elevGradSites$elev_effect_s)

# pathogen damage model with composite elevation variable
pathogen_damage <- lmer(site_logit_pathogen ~ elev_effect
              + SLA_scaled + Height_scaled + log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) ,
              data = df_elevGradSites)

(2) Checking model assumptions of the single models

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
# Define a function for residuals vs fitted + QQ plot
check_model <- function(model, model_name = "") {
  par(mfrow = c(1,2))
  
  # Residuals vs Fitted
  plot(fitted(model), resid(model),
       xlab = "Fitted values", ylab = "Residuals",
       main = paste(model_name, "Residuals vs Fitted"))
  lines(lowess(fitted(model), resid(model)), col = "red", lwd = 2)
  
  # QQ plot
  qqnorm(resid(model), main = paste(model_name, "QQ-Plot"))
  qqline(resid(model), col = "red", lwd = 2)
  
  par(mfrow = c(1,1))  # reset layout
}


models <- list(SLA = SLA, Height = Height, Phenolics = Phenolics, pathogen_damage = pathogen_damage)

for (nm in names(models)) {
  check_model(models[[nm]], nm)
}
# herbivory model
simres <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = herbivory, n = 1000)
plot(simres) # looks good!
# pathogen model
simres_path <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = pathogen_damage, n = 1000)
plot(simres_path) # looks not that good....
par(mfrow = c(1,1))

(3) Building the piecewise SEM

sem_model_path <- psem(
  SLA,
  Height,
  Phenolics,
  pathogen_damage
)

(4) Model checks

Checking for missing paths

Warning

R gives the warning: Check model convergence: log-likelihood estimates lead to negative Chi-squared!
Since I checked the single models for convergence and they are fine, I will ignore this warning for now. Fishers C is still valid (and is more robust for small/complex models).

HERBIVORY COMPOSITE PSEM

(1) Create trait composite

# run multiple regression
trait_model_herb <- lmer(site_logit_herbivory ~ SLA_scaled + Height_scaled + log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) ,
              data = df_elevGradSites)

summary(trait_model_herb)
Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
lmerModLmerTest]
Formula: 
site_logit_herbivory ~ SLA_scaled + Height_scaled + log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled +  
    (1 | country/Gradient_ID)
   Data: df_elevGradSites

REML criterion at convergence: 41.7

Scaled residuals: 
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-1.1759 -0.7188 -0.3101  0.7090  1.5003 

Random effects:
 Groups              Name        Variance Std.Dev.
 Gradient_ID:country (Intercept) 0.07891  0.2809  
 country             (Intercept) 0.03950  0.1987  
 Residual                        0.39286  0.6268  
Number of obs: 19, groups:  Gradient_ID:country, 6; country, 4

Fixed effects:
                            Estimate Std. Error      df t value Pr(>|t|)   
(Intercept)                  -3.7469     0.2182  1.8972 -17.174  0.00424 **
SLA_scaled                    0.2248     0.1996 14.3344   1.126  0.27851   
Height_scaled                 0.2985     0.2045 13.6821   1.460  0.16699   
log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled  -0.1845     0.2191 11.9421  -0.842  0.41635   
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Correlation of Fixed Effects:
            (Intr) SLA_sc Hght_s
SLA_scaled  -0.019              
Height_scld  0.063 -0.124       
lg_Phnylpr_ -0.008  0.409  0.421
# extract coefficients
beta_sla <- summary(trait_model_herb)$coefficients["SLA_scaled", "Estimate"]
beta_height <- summary(trait_model_herb)$coefficients["Height_scaled", "Estimate"]
beta_couamrins <- summary(trait_model_herb)$coefficients["log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled", "Estimate"]

trait_composite <- (beta_sla * df_elevGradSites$SLA_scaled) +
  (beta_height * df_elevGradSites$Height_scaled) +
  (beta_couamrins * df_elevGradSites$log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled)

# add trait_composite variable to the dataframe and standardize it
df_elevGradSites$trait_composite <- trait_composite
df_elevGradSites$trait_composite_s <- scale(trait_composite)
df_elevGradSites$trait_composite_s <- as.numeric(df_elevGradSites$trait_composite_s)

(2) Fitting the individual models

herbivory_traitComp <- lmer(site_logit_herbivory ~ elevation_scaled
              + trait_composite_s + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) ,
              data = df_elevGradSites)
traitsElev <- lmer(trait_composite_s ~ elevation_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) , data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

(3) Checking model assumptions for single models

# herbivory model with trait composite
simres_herb_traitComp <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = herbivory_traitComp, n = 1000)
plot(simres_herb_traitComp)

(4) Building the piecewise SEM

psem_model_traitComp <- psem(
  traitsElev,
  herbivory_traitComp
)

(5) Model checks

Checking for missing paths

dSep(psem_model_traitComp)
data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows

Checking model fit

fisherC(psem_model_traitComp)
  Fisher.C df P.Value
1       NA  0      NA

(6) Model summary

summary(psem_model_traitComp)

Structural Equation Model of psem_model_traitComp 

Call:
  trait_composite_s ~ elevation_scaled
  site_logit_herbivory ~ elevation_scaled + trait_composite_s

    AIC
 100.563

---
Tests of directed separation:

 No independence claims present. Tests of directed separation not possible.

--
Global goodness-of-fit:

Chi-Squared = 0 with P-value = 1 and on 0 degrees of freedom
Fisher's C = NA with P-value = NA and on 0 degrees of freedom

---

  site_logit_herbivory  elevation_scaled  -0.0138    0.2583 10.5036    -0.0534
  site_logit_herbivory trait_composite_s   0.5689    0.2447 15.7543     2.3248
  P.Value Std.Estimate    
   0.0004      -0.8433 ***
   0.9584      -0.0160

  site_logit_herbivory   none     0.40        0.57

PATHOGEN COMPOSITE SEM

(1) Create composite variables for traits

For traits

# run multiple regression
trait_model_path <- lmer(site_logit_pathogen ~ SLA_scaled + Height_scaled + log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) ,
              data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
summary(trait_model_path)

site_logit_pathogen ~ SLA_scaled + Height_scaled + log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled +  
    (1 | country/Gradient_ID)

Scaled residuals:

 country             (Intercept) 2.45     1.565

                            Estimate Std. Error      df t value Pr(>|t|)  
(Intercept)                  -4.5368     0.8568  1.5234  -5.295   0.0585 .
SLA_scaled                   -0.2259     0.4396 13.4019  -0.514   0.6157  
Height_scaled                 0.6182     0.4259 12.7048   1.452   0.1708  
log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled  -0.4379     0.4673 11.8125  -0.937   0.3675

Correlation of Fixed Effects:
            (Intr) SLA_sc Hght_s
SLA_scaled  -0.024

lg_Phnylpr_  0.005  0.302  0.452
optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
# extract coefficients
beta_sla_p <- summary(trait_model_path)$coefficients["SLA_scaled", "Estimate"]
beta_height_p <- summary(trait_model_path)$coefficients["Height_scaled", "Estimate"]
beta_Phenolics_p <- summary(trait_model_path)$coefficients["log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled", "Estimate"]

trait_composite_path <- (beta_sla_p * df_elevGradSites$SLA_scaled) +
  (beta_height_p * df_elevGradSites$Height_scaled) +
  (beta_Phenolics_p * df_elevGradSites$log_Phenylpropanoids_scaled)

# add trait_composite variable to the dataframe
df_elevGradSites$trait_composite_path <- trait_composite_path
# add scaled trait composite variable to the dataframe
df_elevGradSites$trait_composite_path_s <- scale(trait_composite_path)
df_elevGradSites$trait_composite_path_s <- as.numeric(df_elevGradSites$trait_composite_path_s)

(2) Fitting the individual models

traitselev_path <- lmer(trait_composite_path_s ~ elevation_scaled + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) , data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
pathogen_traitComp_elev <- lmer(site_logit_pathogen ~ trait_composite_path_s + elev_effect_s + (1 | country/Gradient_ID) ,
              data = df_elevGradSites)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
Warning: Model failed to converge with 1 negative eigenvalue: -8.2e-02

(3) Checking model assumptions for single models

# pathogen model with trait composite
simres_traitselev_path <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = traitselev_path, n = 1000)
plot(simres_traitselev_path)
simres_path_traitComp <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = pathogen_traitComp_elev, n = 1000)
plot(simres_path_traitComp)

(4) Building the piecewise SEM

# fit psem
psem_model_traitComp_elevcomp <- psem(
  traitselev_path,
  pathogen_traitComp_elev
  #lm(elev_comp_path_s ~ elevation_scaled, data = df_elevGradSites) # simulate correlation of the elevational terms
)

(5) Model checks

Checking for missing paths

dSep(psem_model_traitComp_elevcomp)

  |                                                                            
  |                                                                      |   0%
  |                                                                            
  |===================================                                   |  50%

1 site_logit_pathogen ~ elevation_scaled + ...      coef 12.33117  0.7264237
2 trait_composite_path_s ~ elev_effect_s + ...      coef 13.44830  2.6369638
     P.Value  
1 0.48114143

Checking model fit

fisherC(psem_model_traitComp_elevcomp)
boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
  Fisher.C df P.Value
1    9.282  4   0.054

Refit model due to significant d-sep

# fit psem
psem_model_traitComp_elevcomp <- psem(
  traitselev_path,
  pathogen_traitComp_elev,
  lm(elev_effect_s ~ elevation_scaled, data = df_elevGradSites) # simulate correlation of the elevational terms
)

(6) Model summary
